mnemonics: RAT Runs Across Threads: Remember the main ways to create a thread (Runnable interface, Anonymous class, Thread class extension)
keyPoints: A Thread is a lightweight sub-process that allows concurrent execution of code.
Threads can be created by extending the Thread class or implementing the Runnable interface.
The synchronized keyword is used to prevent thread interference and memory consistency errors.
commonMistakes: Not synchronizing access to shared resources, leading to race conditions.
Calling run() method directly instead of start(), which doesn't create a new thread.
